llm_response:
Subject: Regarding Your Vacation Request

Dear Team Member,

Thank you for submitting your formal request for upcoming time off. We have carefully reviewed your application and appreciate the advance notice you provided regarding your planned absence. Please know that we understand the importance of maintaining a healthy work-life balance for all employees.

We value your contributions to the team and recognize that taking time to recharge is essential for your long-term success and well-being. However, upon further review of our current project timelines and resource allocation, we are unable to approve the specific number of days you requested at this time. We encourage you to consider a shorter duration that aligns better with our immediate operational needs while still allowing you to rest.

To assist you in planning an alternative schedule, please review the following options:
*   Adjust your request to include only two consecutive days off.
*   Split your time off into two separate one-day blocks.
*   Coordinate with your manager to ensure coverage before finalizing any changes.

For more information on our annual leave policy, please visit our internal handbook at [Employee Handbook](https://example.com/handbook). We look forward to discussing a revised plan that works for both you and the department.

Sincerely,

Management Team
